title: The Cost Accountants Act, 1959
full_text: The Cost Accountants Act, 1959 Rules Regulations Notifications Orders Circulars Ordinance Statutes SectionsScheduleAnnexureAppendixFormsActdetails Act ID: 195923 Act Number: 23 Enactment Date: 1959-05-19 Act Year: 1959 Short Title: The Cost Accountants Act, 1959 Long Title: An Act to make provision for the regulation of the profession of cost and works accountants. Ministry: Ministry of Corporate Affairs Enforcement Date: 28-05-1959 Notification: 28th May, 1959, vide notification No. G.S.R. 610, dated 25th May, 1959, see Gazette of India, Extraordinry, Part II, sec. 3(i). Schedule 1. THE FIRST SCHEDULE Order Appendix Forms Schedule 2. THE SECOND SCHEDULE. Order Appendix Forms TitleFiles TitleFiles TitleFiles Show all section The Cost Accountants Act, 1959 CHAPTER I PRELIMINARY CHAPER II THE INSTITUTE OF COST ACCOUNTANTS CHAPTER III COUNCIL OF THE INSTITUTE CHAPTER IV REGISTER OF MEMBERS CHAPTER V MISCONDUCT CHAPTER VI REGIONAL COUNCIL CHAPTER VII PENALTIES CHAPTER VIIA QUALITY REVIEW BOARD CHAPTER VIII DISSSOLUTION OF THE INSTITUTE OF COST AND WORKS ACCOUNTANTS REGISTERED UNDER THE COMPANIES ACT, 1956 (1 OF 1956) CHAPTER IX MISCELLANEOUS Section 1. Short title, extent and commencement. Show Related Subordinates Section 2. Definitions and interpretation. Section 3. Incorporation of the Institute. Show Related Subordinates Section 4. Entry of names in the Register of members. Show Related Subordinates Section 5. Fellows and Associates. Section 6. Certificate of practice. Section 7. Members to be known as cost accountants. Section 8. Disabilities. Show Related Subordinates Section 9. Constitution of the Council of the Institute. Show Related Subordinates Section 10. Re-election or re-nomination to Council. Section 10A. Settlement of disputes regarding election. Section 10B. Establishment of Tribunal. Show Related Subordinates Section 11. Nomination in default of election. Section 12. President and Vice-president. Section 13. Resignation of membership and casual vacancies. Section 14. Duration and dissolution of Council. Section 15. Functions of the Council. Section 15A. Functions of Institute. Section 15B. Imparting education by Universities and other bodies. Section 16. Officers and employees, salary, allowances, etc. Section 17. Committees of the Council. Section 18. Finances of the Council. Section 19. Register of members. Section 20. Removal from the Register of members. Section 21. Disciplinary Directorate. Show Related Subordinates Section 21A. Board of Discipline. Section 21B. Disciplinary Committee. Show Related Subordinates Section 21C. Authority, Disciplinary Committee, Board of Discipline and Director (Discipline) to have powers of civil court. Section 21D. Transitional provisions. Section 22. Professional or other misconduct defined. Section 22A. Constitution of Appellate Authority. Show Related Subordinates Section 22B. Term of office of members of Authority. Section 22C. Procedure, etc., of Authority. Show Related Subordinates Section 22D. Officers and other staff of Authority. Section 22E. Appeal to Authority. Section 23. Constitution and functions of Regional Councils. Section 24. Penalty for falsely claiming to be a member, etc. Section 25. Penalty for using name of the Council, awarding degrees of cost accountancy, etc. Section 26. Companies not to engage in cost accountancy. Section 27. Unqualified persons not to sign documents. Section 28. Offences by companies. Show Related Subordinates Section 29. Sanction to prosecute. Show Related Subordinates Section 29A. Establishment of Quality Review Board. Show Related Subordinates Section 29B. Functions of Board. Show Related Subordinates Section 29C. Procedure of Board. Show Related Subordinates Section 29D. Terms and conditions of service of Chairperson and members of Board and its expenditure. Show Related Subordinates Section 30. Dissolution of the Institute of Cost and Works Accountants registered under the Companies Act, 1956. Section 31. Transfer of assets and liabilities of the dissolved company to the Institute. Section 32. Provisions respecting employees of the dissolved company. Section 33. Omitted. Section 34. Alteration in the Register and cancellation of certificate. Section 35. Directions of the Central Government. Section 36. Protection of action taken in good faith. Section 36A. Members, etc., to be public servants. Section 37. Maintenance of branch offices. Show Related Subordinates Section 38. Reciprocity. Show Related Subordinates Section 38A. Power of Central Government to make rules Show Related Subordinates Section 39. Power to make regulations. Section 40. Rules, regulations and notifications to laid before Parliament. Schedule 1. THE FIRST SCHEDULE Order Appendix Forms Schedule 2. THE SECOND SCHEDULE.
